description_long: This is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a built-in record player, featuring a polished wood exterior and a hinged lid that opens to reveal the turntable and tonearm. The front panel displays a large cream-colored speaker grille, a frequency indicator with yellow markings, and two prominent tuning knobs. The radio's control panel is set into a recessed area below the speaker grille, with a glass window protecting the frequency scale and additional controls. The back of the unit is vented with circular holes for heat dissipation and access to internal components, and it includes various input/output ports and manufacturer markings. The overall design is robust and boxy, with rounded corners and a mid-century aesthetic. The object appears to be well-crafted, combining wood, metal, plastic, and glass elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a built-in record player, featuring a hinged lid, speaker grille, tuning knobs, and a frequency display. The design is robust, with a polished wood finish and mid-century styling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid, record player, cream speaker grille, frequency indicator, and tuning knobs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the curved wooden side and partially open lid, with the record player visible inside.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the wooden back panel with ventilation holes, input/output ports, and manufacturer markings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the smooth wooden side and the open lid, with the record player partially visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
